solution:
B-1
Let
\[
a_{n}=\frac{1}{n^{4}} \prod_{i=1}^{2 n}\left(n^{2}+i^{2}\right)^{1 / n}
\]

Then
\[
\log a_{n}=\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{2 n} \log \left(1+\frac{i^{2}}{n^{2}}\right)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{limit}_{n \rightarrow \infty} \log a_{n}=\int_{0}^{2} \log \left(1+x^{2}\right) d x=2 \log 5-4+2 \arctan 2
\]

descriptive_long.solution:
B-1
Let
\[
sequence=\frac{1}{bigcount^{4}} \prod_{inneridx=1}^{2 bigcount}\left(bigcount^{2}+inneridx^{2}\right)^{1 / bigcount}
\]

Then
\[
\log sequence=\frac{1}{bigcount} \sum_{inneridx=1}^{2 bigcount} \log \left(1+\frac{inneridx^{2}}{bigcount^{2}}\right)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{limit}_{bigcount \rightarrow \infty} \log sequence=\int_{0}^{2} \log \left(1+dummyvar^{2}\right) d dummyvar=2 \log 5-4+2 \arctan 2
\]

descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
B-1
Let
\[
marigold=\frac{1}{longitude^{4}} \prod_{i=1}^{2 longitude}\left(longitude^{2}+i^{2}\right)^{1 / longitude}
\]

Then
\[
\log marigold=\frac{1}{longitude} \sum_{i=1}^{2 longitude} \log \left(1+\frac{i^{2}}{longitude^{2}}\right)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{limit}_{longitude \rightarrow \infty} \log marigold=\int_{0}^{2} \log \left(1+moonlight^{2}\right) d moonlight=2 \log 5-4+2 \arctan 2
\]

descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
B-1
Let
\[
constantvalue=\frac{1}{finitevar^{4}} \prod_{wholevalue=1}^{2 finitevar}\left(finitevar^{2}+wholevalue^{2}\right)^{1 / finitevar}
\]

Then
\[
\log constantvalue=\frac{1}{finitevar} \sum_{wholevalue=1}^{2 finitevar} \log \left(1+\frac{wholevalue^{2}}{finitevar^{2}}\right)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{limit}_{finitevar \rightarrow \infty} \log constantvalue=\int_{0}^{2} \log \left(1+infinitevar^{2}\right) d infinitevar=2 \log 5-4+2 \arctan 2
\]

garbled_string.solution:
B-1
Let
\[
vbcksdfo=\frac{1}{qzxwvtnp^{4}} \prod_{hjgrksla=1}^{2 qzxwvtnp}\left(qzxwvtnp^{2}+hjgrksla^{2}\right)^{1 / qzxwvtnp}
\]

Then
\[
\log vbcksdfo=\frac{1}{qzxwvtnp} \sum_{hjgrksla=1}^{2 qzxwvtnp} \log \left(1+\frac{hjgrksla^{2}}{qzxwvtnp^{2}}\right)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{limit}_{qzxwvtnp \rightarrow \infty} \log vbcksdfo=\int_{0}^{2} \log \left(1+zlkmnwyq^{2}\right) d zlkmnwyq=2 \log 5-4+2 \arctan 2
\]

kernel_variant.question:
Evaluate
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{1}{n^{12}} \prod_{i=1}^{4n} \left(n + 3 i\right)^{3/n} .
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
Set
\[
a_n=\frac{1}{n^{12}}\prod_{i=1}^{4n}(n+3i)^{3/n}.
\]
1. Take natural logarithms:
\[
\log a_n=\frac{3}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{4n}\log(n+3i)-12\log n.
\]
2. Factor out n:
\[
\log(n+3i)=\log n+\log\Bigl(1+\tfrac{3i}{n}\Bigr),
\]
so
\[
\log a_n=\frac{3}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{4n}\log n+\frac{3}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{4n}\log\Bigl(1+\tfrac{3i}{n}\Bigr)-12\log n.
\]
The first summation is $(3/n)(4n)\log n=12\log n$, which cancels $-12\log n$.  Thus
\[
\log a_n=\frac{3}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{4n}\log\Bigl(1+\tfrac{3i}{n}\Bigr).
\]
3. Recognize a Riemann sum.  With $x_i=i/n$,
\[
\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{4n}\log(1+3x_i)\longrightarrow\int_{0}^{4}\log(1+3x)\,dx
\]
as $n\to\infty$.  Multiplying by 3 gives
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\log a_n=3\int_{0}^{4}\log(1+3x)\,dx.
\]
4. Evaluate the integral via $u=1+3x$, $du=3dx$:
\[
\int_{0}^{4}\log(1+3x)\,dx=\frac13\int_{1}^{13}\log u\,du=\frac13\bigl[u\log u-u\bigr]_{1}^{13}=\frac13\bigl(13\log13-12\bigr).
\]
Hence
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\log a_n=3\cdot\tfrac13\bigl(13\log13-12\bigr)=13\log13-12,
\]
and exponentiating gives
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{1}{n^{12}}\prod_{i=1}^{4n}(n+3i)^{3/n}=\frac{13^{13}}{e^{12}}.
\]

core_steps: Apply the natural logarithm to turn the product into a sum.
Factor out n^q from each term so every summand becomes log(1 + a·(i/n)^q).
Recognize (k/n)·Σ_{i=1}^{c n} f(i/n) as a Riemann sum → ∫_{0}^{c} log(1 + a x^q) dx.
Evaluate the integral and exponentiate to recover the desired limit.

slot1.description: Constant multiple c determining how far the index i runs (upper limit c·n and hence the integration interval [0, c]).

slot2.description: Common power q applied to both n and i inside the sum-and-integrand: n^q + a·i^q.

slot3.description: Constant coefficient a on the i^q term (gives log(1 + a·(i/n)^q)).

slot4.description: Factor k in the microscopic exponent k/n on each term of the product.

slot5.description: Macroscopic power s = q·c·k on the prefactor n^{s} that must be cancelled outside (here shown as n^{4}).
